cards.title: Dutch Walking Festivals Keep Kids Happy and Healthy
cards.body: Dutch children participate in annual walking festivals, exploring neighborhoods for four nights while exercising and making friends with peers and teachers. This traditional ritual appears genuinely transformative for child wellbeing and social development.

cards.title: Only Children Aren't Weird, Parents Aren't Selfish
cards.body: Only children face persistent stereotypes about being lonely and maladjusted, while their parents endure judgment for having just one child. But this growing population challenges outdated prejudices about family size.

cards.title: Should Kids Have Pensions? Parents Face New Guilt
cards.body: Parents are being encouraged to open self-invested personal pensions for their children, adding to parental financial pressures. The advice raises questions about where money comes from when many struggle with their own retirement savings.

cards.title: Partner's Mother Cruel: Protecting Future Kids
cards.body: When a partner's parent is emotionally harmful, it's reasonable to minimize their contact with future children unless behavior changes significantly. Setting boundaries now protects your family's wellbeing later.

cards.title: Screen Time Summer: Balance Tech with Active Play
cards.body: Struggling to limit kids' summer screen time? A 'play diet' balances technology use with essential social, physical, and creative activities. This approach helps children develop healthy habits during school breaks.

cards.title: Teach Kids Right From Wrong Without Religion
cards.body: Teaching ethical behavior and moral choices to children doesn't require religious beliefs and doesn't have to be complicated. Simple, consistent guidance helps kids develop strong values.

cards.title: Perspective-Taking Builds Friendship Skills in Summer
cards.body: Summer offers perfect opportunities to strengthen children's empathy and social skills through everyday conversations. Simple perspective-taking exercises help kids understand others' viewpoints and build stronger friendships.

cards.title: HPV Jab Cuts Cervical Cancer Death Risk to Near Zero
cards.body: Girls vaccinated against HPV since 2008 now have 'close to zero' risk of cervical cancer death, saving hundreds of lives. This breakthrough demonstrates the life-changing impact of school vaccination programs.

cards.title: Father-Daughter Films Comfort Grieving Daughters
cards.body: Movies featuring strong father-daughter relationships can provide emotional comfort to daughters grieving absent fathers on Father's Day. Watching these narratives helps process loss and connection.

cards.title: When Adult Siblings Grow Apart: How to Reconnect
cards.body: Many siblings drift apart during adulthood due to life changes, distance, or unresolved conflicts. Understanding common reasons for estrangement helps families work toward meaningful reconnection.
